tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

OSINT accounts indicate that 5 vessels were attacked. Saudi and Qatar have confirmed attacks on their tankers. The US has already retaliated against Iran. Now we are watching Iran retaliate.

For those of you who have been reading my daily updates on the Strait of Hormuz, you knew from a week ago that escalation was coming in the Oman lane. We have now seen a material slowdown in inbound VLCCs, which all but guarantees a slowdown in loading for Saudi, UAE, Iraq, and Kuwait. Even Iran's own loading is slowing due to insufficient tankers returning.

To maintain leverage over the Strait of Hormuz, Iran will have to enforce compliance with the Oman lane, which all but guarantees more conflict in the days/weeks ahead. The US can continue with airstrikes, but there are major limits. Just remember that if airstrikes can win wars, Trump wouldn't have needed to sign the MOU. 

In my view, further escalation is the most likely outcome; flows through the Oman lane are going to slow to a halt, and Brent short positioning is at all-time highs.

Powder keg in the making.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic and my inbound tanker table.
